name: networklab

metadata.name: networklab
metadata.summary: CLI-based Virtual Networking Lab Abstraction Layer

documentation:
# Overview

*[netlab](https://netlab.tools)* is bringing infrastructure-as-code concepts to networking labs. You'll describe your high-level network topology and routing design in a YAML file, and the tools in this repository will

* Create *Vagrantfile* configuration file for *VirtualBox* or *libvirt* environment
* Create *containerlab* configuration file
* Create Ansible inventory and configuration file
* Create IPv4 and IPv6 addressing plan and OSPFv2, OSPFv3, EIGRP, IS-IS, and BGP routing design
* Configure IPv4, IPv6, DHCP, DHCPv6, VLANs, VRFs, VXLAN, LLDP, BFD, OSPFv2, OSPFv3, EIGRP, IS-IS, BGP, RIPv2, RIPng, VRRP, LACP, STP, anycast gateways, route maps, prefix lists, AS-path prefix lists, route redistribution, default route origination, MPLS, BGP-LU, L3VPN (VPNv4 + VPNv6), 6PE, EVPN, SR-MPLS, or SRv6 on your lab devices.

Instead of wasting time creating lab topology in a GUI and configuring boring details, you'll start with a lab preconfigured according to your specifications.

Interested? [Read the documentation](https://netlab.tools) and [installation guidelines](https://netlab.tools/install/).

## Releases

The latest release is [release 1.9.2](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/releases/tag/release_1.9.2). It should be pretty stable, but if you encounter bugs, please report them as [GitHub issues](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/issues/new/choose) and use release [1.9.1](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/releases/tag/release_1.9.1) or [1.8.4-post2](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/releases/tag/release_1.8.4-post2).

<!--
If you encounter bugs using release 1.7.x, please downgrade to [1.6.4](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/releases/tag/release_1.6.4) and [open a GitHub issue](https://github.com/ipspace/netlab/issues).
-->

## An Overview of CLI Commands

**netlab up**
: Uses **[netlab create](https://netlab.tools/netlab/create/)** to create configuration files, starts the virtual lab, and uses **[netlab initial](https://netlab.tools/netlab/initial/)** to deploy device configurations, including IP addressing, LLDP, OSPF, BGP, IS-IS, EIGRP, VRRP, VLANs, VRFs, MPLS, SR-MPLS, VXLAN, EVPN and SRv6. [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/up/)

**netlab down**
: Destroys the virtual lab. [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/down/)

**netlab restart**
: Restart and/or reconfigure the virtual lab. [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/restart/)

**netlab config**
: [Applies additional Jinja2 configuration templates](https://netlab.tools/netlab/config/) to network devices.

**netlab collect**
: Using Ansible fact gathering or other device-specific Ansible modules, [collects device configurations](https://netlab.tools/netlab/collect/) and saves them in the specified directory (default: **config**).

**netlab connect**
: Use SSH or **docker exec** to [connect to a lab device](https://netlab.tools/netlab/connect/) using device names, management network IP addresses (**ansible_host**), SSH port, and username/passwords specified in lab topology or *netlab* device defaults.

**netlab exec**
: Use SSH or **docker exec** to [execute a command on one or more network devices](https://netlab.tools/netlab/exec/) using device names, management network IP addresses (**ansible_host**), SSH port, and username/passwords specified in lab topology or *netlab* device defaults.

**netlab capture**
: [Perform packet capture](https://netlab.tools/netlab/capture/) on VM- and container interfaces

**netlab report**
: Creates a report from the transformed lab topology data.  [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/report/)

**netlab graph**
: Creates a lab topology graph description in Graphviz or D2 format. [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/graph/)

**netlab show**
: Display system settings in tabular, text, or YAML format. [More details](https://netlab.tools/netlab/show/)

variables.name: netsim.modules.lag.ID_SET
documentation: populate_lag_id_set -- Collect any user defined lag.ifindex values globally and initialize ID generator

modules.name: netsim.modules.stp
documentation: STP transformation module

methods.name: netsim.modules.stp.STP.node_pre_transform
documentation: Check for device support for globally selected STP protocol variant

methods.name: netsim.modules.stp.STP.node_post_transform
documentation: Check max port_priority values

modules.name: netsim.modules.sr
documentation: SRv6 transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules._dataplane
documentation: Generic data plane routines

modules.name: netsim.modules.mpls
documentation: MPLS (LDP/BGP-LU) transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules.ospf
documentation: OSPF transformation module

functions.name: netsim.modules.ospf.ospf_unnumbered
documentation: We need to set ospf.unnumbered if we happen to have OSPF running over an unnumbered
link -- Arista EOS needs an extra nerd knob to make it work
An interface can be unnumbered if it has the 'unnumbered' flag set or if it
has IPv4 enabled but no IPv4 address (ipv4: true)
While doing that, we also check for the number of neighbors on unnumbered interfaces
and report an error if there are none (in which case the interface should not be unnumbered)
or more than one (in which case OSPF won't work)

modules.name: netsim.modules.vrf
documentation: VRF module

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.get_node_vrf_data
documentation: get_node_vrf_data: an abstraction layer that returns node-level VRF data structure
as it would appear after the node_post_transform hook
You have to use this function instead of 'node or global data' logic whenever you need
node VRF data before VRF node_post_transform hook is executed

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.init_vrf_static_ids
documentation: Initialize global data structures needed for ID/RD allocation

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.populate_vrf_static_ids
documentation: Populate the global ID/RD/RT data structures with preconfigured global- and node VRF data

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.get_rd_as_number
documentation: Get a usable AS number. Try bgp.as then vrf.as from node and global settings

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.parse_rdrt_value
documentation: Parse rd/rt value -- check whether the RD/RT value is in N:N format

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.normalize_vrf_dict
documentation: Normalize VRF IDs -- give a set of VRFs, change integer values of RDs into N:N strings
The data type sanity checks have been done by topology/node validation module:
* the 'vrfs' attribute is a dictionary of VRF definitions (dictionaries)
* the keys (VRF names) are valid identifiers
This function performs semantic validation

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.get_vrf_id
documentation: Get VRF RD value needed for import/export values.
WARNING: Global value takes precedence over node value because you might want to change per-node RD
values for weird topologies like hub-and-spoke

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.set_vrf_ids
documentation: Set RD values for all VRFs that have no RD attribute or RD value set to None (= auto-generate)

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.set_import_export_rt
documentation: Set import/export route targets

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.validate_vrf_route_leaking
documentation: VRF route leaking is usually implemented through BGP VPNv4 address families
Check whether we have BGP AS configured on all nodes that use VRFs with route leaking
(identified as import or export RT not equal to [ RD ])

functions.name: netsim.modules.vrf.fix_vrf_unnumbered
documentation: If we have an in-VRF loopback, fix parent interface for VRF IPv4 unnumbered interfaces

methods.name: netsim.modules.vrf.VRF.module_pre_transform
documentation: Note: validation of 'vrfs' dictionary has already been done during top-level element validation

methods.name: netsim.modules.vrf.VRF.node_pre_transform
documentation: Note: validation of 'nodes.x.vrfs' dictionary has already been done during node validation

methods.name: netsim.modules.vrf.VRF.node_post_link_transform
documentation: The post-link-transform hook must normalize VRF data and pull global VRF data into
nodes so we can use the node VRF data in copy_node_data_into_interfaces module function
We have to iterate over all VRF interfaces, validate VRF names (which was previously done
in post-transform hook), and populate node VRF data (moved from post-transform hook)

modules.name: netsim.modules.dhcp
documentation: DHCP module

classes.name: netsim.modules.dhcp.DHCP
documentation:
Before going into link transformation, find IPv4 and IPv6 interface addresses set to DHCP,
remove them, and add DHCP to parent node modules.

modules.name: netsim.modules.srv6
documentation: SRv6 transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules.eigrp
documentation: IS-IS transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules.vxlan
documentation: VXLAN module

functions.name: netsim.modules.vxlan.node_set_vtep
documentation: Set VTEP IPv4/IPv6 address

functions.name: netsim.modules.vxlan.build_vtep_list
documentation: Build VLAN-specific VTEP list

methods.name: netsim.modules.vxlan.VXLAN.module_post_transform
documentation: We need multi-step post-transform node handling, so we have to do that in
module_post_transform

modules.name: netsim.modules.isis
documentation: IS-IS transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules.bfd
documentation: BFD transformation module
Contains routines used by routing protocol modules to adjust node/link BFD state

modules.name: netsim.modules
documentation: Dynamic module transformation framework
Individual configuration modules are defined as Python files within this directory inheriting
Module class and adding transformation methods

functions.name: netsim.modules.augment_node_module
documentation: Set default list of modules for nodes without specific module list

functions.name: netsim.modules.check_supported_node_devices
documentation: Check whether the modules defined on individual nodes are valid module names
and supported by the node device type

functions.name: netsim.modules.merge_node_module_params
documentation: Merge global module parameters with per-node module parameters
Remove "no_propagate" values (default: "attributes")
before merging the global settings

functions.name: netsim.modules.get_propagated_global_module_params
documentation: Get propagated global parameters from settings (top-level or device-level)
settings - dictionary with module settings
mod_settings - default module settings

variables.name: netsim.modules.no_propagate_list
documentation:
Return the authoritative list of all modules.

A module is a top-level 'defaults' dictionary key if the value is a dictionary with 'supported_on' key

modules.name: netsim.modules.gateway
documentation: First-hop gateway transformation module

functions.name: netsim.modules.gateway.check_protocol_support
documentation: Check whether a node supports all FHRP protocols configured on it

functions.name: netsim.modules.gateway.cleanup_unicast_ip
documentation: Remove unicast IPv4 addresses from interfaces that use 'anycast' gateway protocol
and have 'gateway.anycast.unicast' set to False

functions.name: netsim.modules.gateway.cleanup_intf_protocol_parameters
documentation: Default settings copied onto individual links have parameters for every known FHRP protocol.
We don't need those parameters on every interface -- this function cleans up unusud gateway protocol
parameters from interfaces and returns a list of active protocols so we know what to clean on the
node level.

classes.name: netsim.modules.gateway.FHRP
documentation:
The main job of the module_init routine is to find potential low-value
gateway.ids and reserve those IDs before they can be used for nodes.

At this point, the data validation and other sanity checks were not done yet,
so we have to be extra-careful accessing the data structures.

modules.name: netsim.modules.bgp
documentation: BGP transformation module

variables.name: netsim.modules.bgp.BGP_DEFAULT_COMMUNITY_KW
documentation: bgp_transform_community_list: transform _netlab_ community keywords into device keywords

classes.name: netsim.modules.bgp.BGP

methods.name: netsim.modules.bgp.BGP.module_post_transform
documentation: Have to set BGP router IDs and cluster IDs before going into node_post_transform

methods.name: netsim.modules.bgp.BGP.node_post_transform
documentation: Execute the rest of node post-transform code, including setting up the BGP session table

Module pre-default:

* process AS list
* create automatic BGP groups

modules.name: netsim.modules.ripv2
documentation: RIPv2/RIPng transformation module

modules.name: netsim.modules.routing
documentation: Generic routing module:
* Routing policies (route maps)
* Routing filters (prefixes, communities, as-paths)
* Static routes

variables.name: netsim.modules.routing.match_kw
documentation: normalize_routing_entry: generic normalization of any list used by the routing module

variables.name: netsim.modules.routing.import_dispatch
documentation: Import or merge global routing policies into node routing policies

variables.name: netsim.modules.routing.normalize_dispatch
documentation: normalize_routing_data: execute the normalization functions for all routing objects

modules.name: netsim.data.filemaps
documentation: This module handles file map - lists of strings in a:b format - that
are used in clab to map files from the host to the container.
We used to store them in boxes, but that stopped being an option with
python-box 7.0 (or so) that started turning dotted keys into hierarchies.

modules.name: netsim.modules._routing
documentation:
Routing Protocol utility functions:

* network_type: set IGP network type on a link. Used by OSPF and IS-IS
* routing_af: set routing protocol address families for a node
* external: return True is an interface is an external interface, and removes IGP-related parameters from the interface
* passive: set IGP 'passive' flag on an interface

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.routing_af
documentation: Build routing protocol address families
* If the address families are not set, calculate them based on interface address families
* Otherwise parse and validate the AF attribute

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.network_type
documentation: Set network type for an interface:
* If the network type is specified, validate it against a list of allowed network types
* Otherwise, set network type to P2P if the interface has two nodes attached to it

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.external
documentation: Remove routing protocol data from an interface with "external" role

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.is_true_stub
documentation: Figure out whether an interface is truly a stub interface
This routing is called to figure out what exactly a link with a 'stub'
role is. When used by IGP modules, it checks whether there's an adjacent
node running the same IGP (in which case the interface should not be passive).
When used by BGP, it just checks whether a neighbor is a daemon

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.get_router_id_prefix
documentation: Get a router ID prefix from the router_id pool

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.router_id
documentation: Create router ID if needed

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.remove_vrf_interfaces
documentation: remove_vrf_interfaces -- remove interfaces in a VRF from a routing process that is not VRF-aware

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.build_vrf_interface_list
documentation: build_vrf_interface_list -- copy VRF interfaces into VRF definition

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.remove_unaddressed_intf
documentation: remove_unaddressed_intf -- remove all interfaces without IPv4 or IPv6 address from IGP
This routing should be called BEFORE build_vrf_interface_list to ensure the interfaces without any usable
L3 address are not copied into VRF interface list

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.remove_unused_igp
documentation: remove_unused_igp -- remove IGP module if it's not configured on any interface

functions.name: netsim.modules._routing.remove_vrf_routing_blocks
documentation: remove_vrf_routing_blocks -- remove 'proto: False' VRF settings

modules.name: netsim.data.validate
documentation: Data validation routines

variables.name: netsim.data.validate.topo_pointer
documentation:
get_attribute_namespaces

Given a list of attribute namespaces, iterate through attributes and extend the list
with potential _namespace definitions.

modules.name: netsim.data
documentation: Generic data model manipulation outines

functions.name: netsim.data.get_box
documentation: I had enough -- here's a function that returns a box with proper default settings

functions.name: netsim.data.get_a_list
documentation: Another thingy we need all the time: make something a list

functions.name: netsim.data.null_to_string
documentation: Change all NULL values in a nested dictionary structure to empty strings
to make them nicer in YAML printouts

functions.name: netsim.data.remove_null_values
documentation: Recursively remove null values from a box

functions.name: netsim.data.get_global_parameter
documentation: Get a global setting or corresponding system default. Use for attributes that are not propagated or early in the
transformation logic when the module attributes haven't been propagated yet
Use 'get_global_parameter' to get a single value and 'get_global_settings' to get a merged dictionary of
default and topology settings
Use 'get_global_settings' when you need a dictionary of global module parameters if the module uses
'no_propagate' flag to stop leaking into nodes.

modules.name: netsim.common
documentation: Import common routines into a single namespace for backward-compatibility reasons

modules.name: netsim.data.global_vars
documentation: Implement global variables hidden within topology defaults
Needed to support reentrant use of netsim modules (custom transformations or tests)

variables.name: netsim.data.global_vars._glob_dict
documentation:
init -- create 'globals' entry in 'topology.defaults' and save a pointer to it

It would be ideal to get a pointer to topology in every call to 'get', but
that's not realistic.

modules.name: netsim.devices.unknown
documentation: Arista EOS quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.cumulus
documentation: Arista EOS quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.iosvl2
documentation: Cisco IOSvL2 quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.asav

modules.name: netsim.modules.vlan
documentation: VLAN configuration module

functions.name: netsim.modules.vlan.routed_access_vlan
documentation: routed_access_vlan: Given a link with access/native VLAN, check if all nodes on the link use routed VLAN

methods.name: netsim.modules.vlan.VLAN.module_pre_transform
documentation: Note: VLAN data has been validated as part topology/node validation before
the module_pre_transform function is called. Is't thus safe to assume
the vlan/vlans data is sane

modules.name: netsim.devices.linux
documentation: Linux quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.eos
documentation: Arista EOS quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.junos
documentation: Juniper JunOS quirks
# Every interface is a subunit ->
# rename interface names from ge-0/0/0 to ge-0/0/0.0 (and so avoid using unit 0 on the initial template)
# the same can apply to loopbacks, irbs, ...
# + if the vlan module is in use, and multiple units are present, set the unit 0 as untagged vlan but with the vlan-id (default 1)

modules.name: netsim.devices.dellos10
documentation: Dell OS10 quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.vsrx
documentation: Juniper JunOS quirks
# Every interface is a subunit ->
# rename interface names from ge-0/0/0 to ge-0/0/0.0 (and so avoid using unit 0 on the initial template)
# the same can apply to loopbacks, irbs, ...
# + if the vlan module is in use, and multiple units are present, set the unit 0 as untagged vlan but with the vlan-id (default 1)

modules.name: netsim.devices.vptx
documentation: Juniper JunOS quirks
# Every interface is a subunit ->
# rename interface names from ge-0/0/0 to ge-0/0/0.0 (and so avoid using unit 0 on the initial template)
# the same can apply to loopbacks, irbs, ...
# + if the vlan module is in use, and multiple units are present, set the unit 0 as untagged vlan but with the vlan-id (default 1)

modules.name: netsim.devices.vmx
documentation: Juniper JunOS quirks
# Every interface is a subunit ->
# rename interface names from ge-0/0/0 to ge-0/0/0.0 (and so avoid using unit 0 on the initial template)
# the same can apply to loopbacks, irbs, ...
# + if the vlan module is in use, and multiple units are present, set the unit 0 as untagged vlan but with the vlan-id (default 1)

modules.name: netsim.devices
documentation: Device quirks -- a framework to deal with devices with 'interesting' implementations
Each device with quirks should have a module file in this directory with a class derived from _Quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.arubacx
documentation: Aruba AOS-CX quirks
# OSPF Process ID can only be 1-63.
# When using VRFs, the process ID is taken from the vrfidx, which usually is > 100.
# Here we are mapping every VRF to a specific ospfidx

modules.name: netsim.devices.iosv
documentation: Cisco IOSv quirks

functions.name: netsim.devices.iosv.check_vrrp_bvi
documentation: Cisco IOSv does not support VRRP on BVI interfaces. Go figure...

modules.name: netsim.data.types
documentation: Data validation routines

functions.name: netsim.data.types.attr_help
documentation: Get attribute help -- return 'use netlab show attributes XYZ' help message

functions.name: netsim.data.types.must_be_ipv4
documentation: Testing for IPv4 and IPv6 addresses is nasty, as netaddr module happily mixes IPv4 and IPv6

variables.name: netsim.data.types._wrong_type_help
documentation: Remember the 'wrong type' hints we printed out

variables.name: netsim.data.types._attr_help_cache
documentation: Remember which attribute help messages we already printed out

modules.name: netsim.devices.ioll2
documentation: Cisco IOSvL2 quirks

modules.name: netsim.devices.srlinux
documentation: SR Linux quirks
# inter-VRF route leaking is only supported in combination with BGP EVPN
# based on IP prefixes, not (currently 24.3.1) on communities

modules.name: netsim
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

modules.name: netsim.devices.vjunos-switch
documentation: Juniper JunOS quirks
# Every interface is a subunit ->
# rename interface names from ge-0/0/0 to ge-0/0/0.0 (and so avoid using unit 0 on the initial template)
# the same can apply to loopbacks, irbs, ...
# + if the vlan module is in use, and multiple units are present, set the unit 0 as untagged vlan but with the vlan-id (default 1)

modules.name: netsim.tools
documentation: Dynamic output framework
Individual output routines are defined in modules within this directory inheriting
TopologyOutput class and replacing or augmenting its methods (most commonly, write)

modules.name: netsim.cli.report

Create a text or HTML report from the current lab topology

functions.name: netsim.cli.report.report_parse

Collect device configurations

functions.name: netsim.cli.collect.initial_config_parse
documentation: CLI parser for 'netlab collect' command

modules.name: netsim.tools.graphite
documentation: Why use Graphite (https://github.com/netreplica/graphite) only with ContainerLab? :-)
With this output module it would be possible to export a topology file to be used within Graphite.
Graphite container can be launched with:
docker run -d \
-v "$(pwd)/graphite-default.json":/var/www/localhost/htdocs/default/default.json \
--name graphite \
netreplica/graphite:webssh2

Creates virtualization provider configuration and automation inventory from

Deploy custom configuration template to network devices

modules.name: netsim.cli._nodeset
documentation:
This module implements nodesets -- a subnet of nodes specified in the
'--node' parameter of some netlab commands.

The nodeset string specification could contain a single element or
multiple elements separated by commas. Each element could be a 
node name or a glob.

functions.name: netsim.cli._nodeset.is_glob
documentation: Is a string a glob expression?

functions.name: netsim.cli._nodeset.add_glob
documentation: Find all names matching a glob expression and append them to the node name list

modules.name: netsim.cli.validate
documentation: netlab validate command

variables.name: netsim.extra.bgp.domain.plugin._requires
documentation:
post_transform hook

We're going to iterate through IBGP neighbors on each node and remove cross-domain IBGP sessions.
At the same time, we're going to keep track of route reflectors for each AS/domain, and whenever
we find one, will validate that each domain-specific copy of an AS has a usable route reflector.

modules.name: netsim.utils.read
documentation: Read topology and default settings files

functions.name: netsim.utils.read.build_defaults_list
documentation: Build the list of defaults
The defaults are taken from defaults.sources.list (if exists) or constructed from
* Extra topology-specific sources (defaults.sources.extra)
* User defaults (defaults.sources.user or API argument or USER_DEFAULTS)
* System defaults (defaults.sources.system or API argument or SYSTEM_DEFAULTS)
The user and system defaults should almost never be used, the viable exceptions are:
* Test harness -- error cases
* netlab show --system command

functions.name: netsim.utils.read.include_environment_defaults
documentation: include_environment_defaults -- set defaults from environment variables
* Consider all environment variables starting with NETLAB_
* Replace underscores with dots, turn string into lowercase
* Evaluate the resulting string and store the results

documentation:
include_yaml: Include YAML snippets at any position within a YAML file

* scan all dictionaries for '_include' key
* every _include value should be a list of files to include

Glob over all files to include:
* try to read YAML file
* add contents of the YAML file as another dictionary entry, using filename
  as the key

variables.name: netsim.providers.libvirt.LIBVIRT_MANAGEMENT_SUBNET
documentation:
Replace management IP subnet in vagrant-libvirt XML template:

* Replace subnet (.1 address) and netmask
* Replace start (.2) and end (start -1) of dynamic DHCP range
* Replace IP addresses in static DHCP bindings (from start until the next address is no longer found)

Replacements have to match single quotes (in XML) to ensure we don't replace partial IP addresses

modules.name: netsim.augment.addressing
documentation:
Set up network addressing
=========================

The network topology needs three types of addressing pools:

* Loopback addresses
* P2P addresses
* LAN and stub link addresses

Each pool could have IPv4 and IPv6 component. Alternatively, a pool
could be unnumbered (using IPv6 LLA and IPv4 address from loopback interface).

Notes:
* _lan_ pool is used for stub and P2P prefixes if the corresponding
  pools are not defined

The addressing pools are set up in _addressing_ part of topology
defaults (local or global), for example:

addressing:
  lan:
    ipv4: 172.16.0.0/16
    ipv6: 2008:db8:0100::/48
    prefix: 24
  p2p:
    ipv4: 10.1.0.0/16
    prefix: 30
  loopback:
    ipv4: 10.0.0.0/24

IPv4 component of an addressing pool is defined with two parameters:

* _ipv4_ prefix - IPv4 addressing space used by the pool
* _prefix_ length - prefix length of individual subnets in the pool

IPv6 component of an addressing pool is defined with an _ipv6_ prefix,
prefix length of individual subnets is assumed to be /64 unless the
pool has _prefix6_ attribute.

Legacy setup migration
----------------------
While building the addressing pools the **setup** routine uses legacy
parameters when the corresponding addressing parameteres are missing:

* _loopback_ default is used to create _loopback_ pool
* _lan_ and _lan_subnet_ defaults are used to create _LAN_ pool
* _p2p_ and _p2p_subnet_ defaults are used to create _p2p_ pool

modules.name: netsim.augment.groups
documentation:
Add group support. This module handles group-related data structures and
data transformation. The end result is a dictionary of groups in 'groups'
top-level element. That dictionary is merged from global- and node-level parameters

variables.name: netsim.augment.links.IPAM_dispatch
documentation:
Assign addresses to all interfaces on a link

* Skip if the link has no usable prefix (l2only link)
* Use IPAM_unnumbered on old-style unnumbered links
* Figure out allocation policies (based on link type, prefix size, number of interfaces)
* Execute selected IPAM routing
